news.title: Relief for some OPT applicants in “immigration limbo”
news.summary_en: A federal judge has ordered the Trump administration to lift an indefinite freeze on work permit authorisations, including OPT, for a group of international students from travel ban countries.
news.author: Polly Nash

news.title: Canada’s language sector looks to joint pathways to rebuild trust and growth
news.summary_en: Canada’s language education sector has been hit hard by a combination of pandemic disruption, policy change and closures, but a new Joint Pathway Program is emerging as a focal point for renewal.
news.author: Kim Martin

news.title: Scholarship targets study abroad gap for US community colleges
news.summary_en: A new scholarship has launched to expand access to study abroad opportunities to community college students in the US – a population starkly underrepresented in global learning initiatives.
news.author: Polly Nash

news.title: How many international students are studying in Australia?
news.summary_en: The PIE compiles the most recent data on the number of international students in Australia, including those in the post-graduate work stream, along with insights into the leading source markets.
news.author: Charlotte Greenstein

news.title: Universities face “rollercoaster” decade as AI tests their value
news.summary_en: AI and rapid technological change are forcing universities to rethink their very purpose, global university leaders have warned, as they discuss what value higher education offers in a world where machines "compete with our brain".
news.author: Kim Martin

news.title: DAAD shifts Indian focus beyond student mobility
news.summary_en: In a conversation with The PIE, senior DAAD officials highlighted a shift in India strategy towards long-term partnerships and research, while tackling one-sided mobility and access gaps.
news.author: Abhishek Nair

news.title: Demand outstrips capacity at South Africa’s universities, warns UJ vice-chancellor
news.summary_en: South Africa’s public universities are facing a "profound contradiction" as demand for higher education surges beyond capacity, University of Johannesburg vice‑chancellor and principal Letlhokwa Mpedi has warned.
news.author: Kim Martin
